description: Opt-in OS notifications with rate limiting for daily summaries

userStories.title: Add notify-rust dependency
userStories.description: Add `notify-rust` to Cargo.toml. Feature-gate it for macOS and Linux only. notify-rust requires `mac_notification_sys` on macOS and `dbus` on Linux. Use `default-features = false` and explicitly enable `images` feature is NOT required. The crate itself handles platform dispatch internally — no cfg-gating needed in lib code beyond the availability check.
acceptanceCriteria: Cargo.toml gains `notify-rust = { version = "4", default-features = false }` in [dependencies]
`cargo build` passes on macOS and Linux
No new modules yet — just the dep

userStories.title: Config: notifications opt-in fields
userStories.description: Add notification config fields to the Config struct in config.rs. All fields have serde defaults so existing configs without these fields continue to work. Notifications are disabled by default (opt-in). The notification_time field specifies when the daily summary notification fires (local time, HH:MM format, 24h). Default is '17:00'.
acceptanceCriteria: `Config` gains `pub notifications_enabled: bool` with `#[serde(default)]` (defaults to false)
`Config` gains `pub notification_time: String` with `#[serde(default = "default_notification_time")]`
`fn default_notification_time() -> String { "17:00".to_string() }` added near other default fns
`Config::default()` updated: `notifications_enabled: false`, `notification_time: default_notification_time()`
Config TOML round-trips correctly: serialize then deserialize yields same values
`cargo build` passes
No interactive prompts added to run_init() — users opt in by manually editing config

userStories.title: notifications.rs: wrapper module with send_notification()
userStories.description: Create src/notifications.rs. Provides a single public function `send_notification(title: &str, body: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` that calls notify-rust. Handles the 'notification permission denied' and 'no notification server' error cases by returning Ok(()) with a warn log rather than propagating — callers must not crash if notifications are unavailable. Also exposes `is_available() -> bool` using a OnceLock probe pattern (same pattern as enrichment.rs).
acceptanceCriteria: `src/notifications.rs` created

`pub fn send_notification(title: &str, body: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — calls `notify_rust::Notification::new().summary(title).body(body).show()`, maps errors to anyhow, logs warn on error, returns Ok(())

`pub fn is_available() -> bool` — uses `OnceLock<bool>`, probes by sending a silent test (or just returns `true` on macOS since no permission denial at runtime, `false` on headless — detect via `std::env::var("DISPLAY").is_ok() || cfg!(target_os = "macos")`)
Module declared in lib.rs: `pub mod notifications;`
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: DB: notification_log table for rate limiting
userStories.description: Add a `notification_log` table to track sent notifications. Prevents re-firing the daily summary if the daemon restarts mid-day. The key is a date string (YYYY-MM-DD) + notification type. Uses INSERT OR IGNORE to make repeated sends idempotent.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration in db.rs `open_db()` migrations array: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_log (date TEXT NOT NULL, notification_type TEXT NOT NULL, sent_at TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (date, notification_type));`
`pub fn notification_was_sent(conn: &Connection, date: &str, notification_type: &str) -> anyhow::Result<bool>` in db.rs — SELECT EXISTS query
`pub fn record_notification_sent(conn: &Connection, date: &str, notification_type: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` in db.rs — INSERT OR IGNORE with sent_at = Utc::now().to_rfc3339()
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: query.rs: daily_summary_for_notification()
userStories.description: Add a query function that returns a compact notification body string for today's activity. Format: 'N commits across R repos — ~Xh Ym'. Uses existing query_activity() and global_estimated_time() functions. Returns None if no activity (no notification needed).
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn daily_summary_for_notification(conn: &Connection, session_gap_minutes: u64, first_commit_minutes: u64) -> anyhow::Result<Option<String>>` in query.rs
Uses `today_range()` to get (from, to), calls `query_activity(conn, from, to, ...)` to get repos
If repos is empty AND total_commits == 0, returns Ok(None)
Computes global_estimated_time from repos
Returns Ok(Some(body)) where body = format: '{commits} commit{s} across {repos} repo{s} — ~{hours}h {mins}m' (omit hours if 0, omit mins if 0, handle singular/plural)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: poller.rs: daily summary notification trigger
userStories.description: In run_poll_loop(), after each full_scan() call, check if the daily summary notification should fire. Conditions: notifications_enabled=true, is_available()=true, current local time >= notification_time, notification not yet sent today (check DB). If all conditions met: query daily summary, send notification, record in DB.
acceptanceCriteria: After each full_scan() call in run_poll_loop(), call a new `maybe_send_daily_notification(config, &conn)` function

`fn maybe_send_daily_notification(config: &Config, conn: &Connection)` in poller.rs (private): checks config.notifications_enabled, calls notifications::is_available(), parses config.notification_time as HH:MM, compares against Local::now().time()
Checks DB: `notification_was_sent(conn, &today_date, "daily_summary")` — if already sent, return early

If time >= notification_time and not sent: calls `daily_summary_for_notification(conn, ...)` — if Some(body), calls `notifications::send_notification("Blackbox Daily Summary", &body)` and `record_notification_sent(conn, &today_date, "daily_summary")`
All errors logged at warn level, never propagated (function returns void)
notification_time parse failure → log warn + skip (don't panic)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Unit tests: notification_log DB helpers
userStories.description: Tests for notification_was_sent() and record_notification_sent() in tests/db_test.rs (or new tests/notifications_test.rs).
acceptanceCriteria: Test: notification_was_sent on empty table returns Ok(false)
Test: record_notification_sent then notification_was_sent returns Ok(true)
Test: record_notification_sent twice (same date+type) — second call is Ok(()) and doesn't error (INSERT OR IGNORE)
Test: different date → notification_was_sent returns Ok(false) for the un-sent date
Test: different notification_type same date → returns Ok(false) for un-sent type
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: daily_summary_for_notification()
userStories.description: Tests for the query function that builds the notification body string.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: no activity in DB → returns Ok(None)
Test: insert 3 commit events in one repo today → returns Ok(Some(body)) where body contains '3 commit' and '1 repo'
Test: insert commits across 2 repos → body contains '2 repo'
Test: singular 'commit'/'repo' when count=1
Test: time estimate > 0 appears in body in h/m format
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: maybe_send_daily_notification() logic
userStories.description: Tests for the notification trigger logic. Because send_notification() calls a real OS API, use a test config with notifications_enabled=false or mock the time check. Focus on the conditional logic, not the actual OS notification.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: notifications_enabled=false → maybe_send_daily_notification does nothing (no DB write)
Test: notifications_enabled=true, notification_time in future (23:59) → does not write to DB
Test: notifications_enabled=true, notification_time in past (00:01), no activity → does not write to DB (daily_summary returns None)
Test: notification already recorded in DB → does not attempt to send again (idempotency)
Tests use tempfile DB and Config::default() with fields overridden
All tests pass

userStories.title: Integration test: notifications disabled by default
userStories.description: CLI integration test confirming opt-in behavior — blackbox today and blackbox start don't error even with no notification config.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: Config::default() has notifications_enabled=false
Test: Config round-trip through TOML (serialize/deserialize) preserves notifications_enabled=false and notification_time='17:00' when those fields are absent from TOML
Test: TOML with `notifications_enabled = true` and `notification_time = "09:30"` deserializes correctly
All tests pass

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
